blockers.mitigation: Audit 2026-06-27 found entry/src/main/cpp/types/libreader_core_napi/Index.d.ts only declares 3 exports (abiVersion / pingSmoke / sendJsonCommand) while reader_napi.cpp exports 12 (abiVersion / createRuntime / releaseRuntime / sendCommand / cancelRequest / readEvent / pendingEventCount / completeHostRequest / failHostRequest / pingSmoke / hostSmoke / lifecycleSmoke). sendJsonCommand is a stale POC residual not present in C++. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-27-harmonyos-rust-core-integration-s6.md Task 1 rewrites Index.d.ts to declare all 12 exports aligned with bindings/harmony/sdk/reader_core.ts NativeReaderCoreModule type. Blocks: any ArkTS code referencing completeHostRequest/failHostRequest/readEvent/pendingEventCount/hostSmoke/lifecycleSmoke will not compile or runtime-link against libreader_core_napi.so. Verification: hvigorw assembleHap + ReaderCoreNapiBridge.ets runHostSmokeClosedLoop() green on simulator.
blockers.reason: HarmonyOS NAPI Index.d.ts severely stale (3/12 exports declared, includes non-existent sendJsonCommand); breaks NAPI smoke closed-loop validation and any host.request round-trip from ArkTS

blockers.mitigation: Audit 2026-06-27 found no ArkTS host-adapter module exists in entry/src/main/ets/. Android has bindings/android/host-adapter/src/main/java/com/reader/core/host/ (HostAdapter.java + HostTransport + HostReply + HostReplyCodec + CapabilityHandler) but HarmonyOS has no equivalent. BridgeHTTPClient.ets implements 'Strategy B: localhost:8899 dev bridge' which bypasses Core host.request protocol entirely and violates Core/Host boundary. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-27-harmonyos-rust-core-integration-s6.md Task 3 creates entry/src/main/ets/hostadapter/ module (10 files: HostTransport / HttpRequest / HttpResponse / HostReply / HostReplyCodec / CapabilityHandler / HostAdapter / HostBus / HttpExecuteHandler / HostRuntime) mirroring Android host-adapter. Task 4 writes OHOSHTTPHostTransport using @ohos.net.http. Blocks: any real host.request round-trip (HTTP transport, persistence, system TTS) cannot route from Core to Host capabilities. Verification: host.request('http.execute', ...) returns host.complete with real 2xx body on simulator.
blockers.reason: HarmonyOS missing ArkTS host-adapter module; Core host.request events cannot be dispatched to platform-owned capabilities (HTTP / persistence / TTS). Current BridgeHTTPClient bypasses Core protocol entirely.

blockers.mitigation: Audit 2026-06-27 found all 5 ArkTS pages (Index.ets Tab container + BookshelfPage / SearchPage / ReaderPage / SettingsPage) routed through fixture-based services. HomeDashboardService uses MockBookshelfRepository(true) + FixtureReplayInterceptor + 3 fixture:// sources. SearchService uses BridgeClientWithFallback(BridgeHTTPClient, FixtureReplayInterceptor). ReaderPage uses FixtureEPUBParserAdapter + buildStoredEPUBFixture() with hardcoded content '在中国，任何超脱飞扬的思想...'. SettingsPage has no 'import source' entry. None of the 5 pages call ReaderCoreRuntime.send/request. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-27-harmonyos-rust-core-integration-s6.md Task 5 creates ReaderCoreClient App singleton + BookApi/SourceApi facade, Task 6 retires fixture services (strangler pattern: deprecate not delete), Task 7 modifies 5 pages' ViewModel layer to consume RustCore*Service. Blocks: HarmonyOS cannot pass Legado source search→detail→toc→content vertical via Rust Core. Verification: RustCoreEndToEndTest asserts non-fixture path + real Legado source pipeline green on simulator.
blockers.reason: HarmonyOS 5-page UI business path completely not connected to Rust Core; all pages route through MockBookshelfRepository / FixtureReplayInterceptor / BridgeClientWithFallback / FixtureEPUBParserAdapter. Legado search→detail→toc→content cannot run via Rust Core.
